_comment: Grand Concert ('Our Grand Concert') SONG catalogue.
Full song list imported from https://uma.guide/guides/grand-concert and
cross-validated against live captures: 7 of 7 songs seen in game matched the
guide EXACTLY on cost and effects, so the remaining rows are trusted.
SCOPE - songs only. Techniques are deliberately not catalogued; see the
'techniques' section for why, and for the rules the purchase logic needs.
RUNTIME CONTRACT
Read the offered lesson's name, cost and affordability off the SHOP SCREEN
every visit. This file supplies the strategic prior - 'priority' - which the
screen cannot tell us, plus canonical costs for multi-turn planning.
Song costs have proven stable per song ('Go This Way' identical across three
appearances), so planning against 'cost' is sound; techniques are the
opposite and must always be read live.
CURRENCY NAMES - the guide labels the Visual currency 'Guts' (the stat it
feeds). Costs here use the in-game chip codes:
Da Dance | Pa Passion | Vo Vocal | Vi Visual | Co Composure
EFFECT WORDING - the guide and the game word effects differently. Match on the
GAME's text when parsing a card:
guide 'Speed Training +1'        -> game 'Training Speed Gain +1'
guide 'Friendship Bonus +5%'     -> game 'Friendship Training Effectiveness +5%'
guide 'Speciality Priority Up +5'-> game 'Specialty Priority +5'
guide 'Support Chain Event Frequency +1' -> game 'Support Chain Event Frequency Lvl +1'
PRIORITY - verbatim from the guide: High Priority / Great Value / Good Value /
Low Priority / Special. The ordering between 'High Priority' and 'Great Value'
is NOT stated by the guide; Phase 2 must make that judgement call rather than
assume this list is ranked.
Effect 'kind' values:
stat            - immediate flat stat gain ('Speed +22')
all_stats       - flat gain to every stat
skill_pts       - immediate skill point gain
all_points      - flat gain to every performance point currency
training_gain   - persistent training bonus ('Training Speed Gain +1')
skill_pt_bonus  - persistent skill point bonus
specialty_priority, support_chain_freq, friendship_training_effectiveness
- concert bonuses; apply only after the next concert
Only SONGS raise the Hype gauge (confirmed: learning a technique did not move it).
Techniques are intentionally not catalogued. Their mastery effects are small
and interchangeable (+5 to a stat, +5 skill points, one skill hint), so
knowing which technique is on offer does not change what the bot should do.
The guide likewise gives only categories, not individual technique rows.
Observed effects: Speed/Stamina/Power/Wit +5; Skill Pts +5; Skill Hint Lvl +1
(distance varies per offer); and from a mockup, Guts +12 - so higher tiers
with larger effects exist. The guide singles out ENERGY techniques as the
best pick; none seen yet, so special-case one if it appears.

cost_rules.stat_basics: 10 of the currency matching the stat raised - Speed/Da, Stamina/Pa, Power/Vo, Wit/Co. Confirmed 4 of 5 stats; Guts/Vi unseen. Keys off the stat, NOT the lesson name ('Audience Involvement Basics' costs Passion).
cost_rules.skill_hint: 15 of a single currency, which varies per offer.
cost_rules.skill_pts: 10 of a single currency, which varies per offer.
cost_rules.tiers: Amount scales with tier at a fixed ratio of 2x the stat gain: Basics +5 -> 10, Intermediate +8 -> 16, dual +4/+4 -> 8+8. ('Makeup Advanced Class' +12 would imply 24, unverified.) Energy +20 cost Vi 25, which does not follow the 2x rule - energy has no stat, so both its currency and price look independent.
cost_rules.dual_stat: A dual-stat technique costs BOTH matching currencies: 'Isolation Basics' (Speed +4, Wit +4) cost Da 8 + Co 8. The stat->currency rule extends cleanly to multi-stat techniques.

techniques.costs_and_effects_vary_per_offer: Same technique, different offer: 'Watch an Up-and-Coming Idol's Concert' Vo10 -> Da10; 'Group Lesson Basics' Da15 -> Co15 AND its hint changed (Medium) -> (Mile). Always read live.
purchase_logic_notes: Buying is the ONLY way to roll a new offer set - waiting never rotates it.
So when a set holds no song worth taking, buying a technique is the
mechanism for rolling toward one, not merely a consolation purchase.
Prefer the technique that spends currency the songs you want do NOT need.
Songs cost 21-42 of specific currencies, so draining a song currency on a
10-15 point technique can delay a song by several turns.

energy_techniques.why: The guide rates energy techniques the best pick (they avoid a rest turn). CONFIRMED in game: 'Facial-Slimming Massage' grants Energy +20 for Vi 25 - a whole turn's worth of energy for the price of a mid-tier technique.
energy_techniques.detection_primary: OCR the mastery row (img[y0+50:y0+112, 200:680]) and test for 'energy' case-insensitively. The row must be read anyway to know what a technique does, so this is nearly free. Verified: reads 'Energy +20'.
energy_techniques.detection_corroborating: The category icon (img[y0+64:y0+184, 63:183]) is BLUE for energy and warm for everything else. Measured over 12 technique icons: energy = median hue 102 with 97.1% blue pixels; all 11 others = hue 18-46 with <=1.2% blue. Test: saturated pixels with hue 90-130 exceeding ~50% of the icon. Use as a second signal so a single OCR slip cannot mis-skip an energy technique.
energy_techniques.icon_hue_also_encodes_category: Observed medians: ~19 orange = flat stat, ~46 green = skill hint, ~102 blue = energy. Consistent with the guide's mention of category icons; could classify other categories later if useful.

techniques.mastery_row_can_hold_multiple_effects: 'Isolation Basics' showed two effects stacked in one mastery row; OCR returns them concatenated ('Speed +4Wit +4'). Split on the +N boundaries when parsing.

songs.name: Run n' Run!
songs.notes: Only THREE-currency song in the start tier; also the cheapest per-currency, so often affordable early. NAME MISMATCH: the guide lists this as 'RUNxRUN'; Global shows 'Run n' Run!'. Cost and effects match exactly.

songs.name: Full Speed Ahead! Umadol Power☆
songs.notes: In-game name carries a trailing star glyph the guide omits; similarity 0.984, so fuzzy matching is safe.

songs.name: Zero Is Where the Center Stands!
songs.guide_name: Zero is Where the Center Stands

songs.name: Go This Way
songs.notes: Three appearances, identical cost every time - the evidence that song costs are stable per song.

songs.name: Getaway! Fallin' Love
songs.notes: NAME MISMATCH: the guide calls it 'Run Away! Fallin' Love'; the game shows 'Getaway! Fallin' Love'. Cost and effects match exactly, so it is the same song - match on the in-game name.

songs.name: Run For Our Dream
songs.notes: Guide advises buying this immediately for the cumulative skill point bonus.

songs.name: Hey, Guess What!
songs.notes: Name uses interpuncts; OCR may render them as spaces or dots. Match loosely. NAME MISMATCH: the guide lists this as 'A・NO・NE'; Global shows 'Hey, Guess What!'. Cost and effects match exactly.

songs.name: Grow Up and Shine!
songs.notes: NAME MISMATCH: guide lists 'Grow Up, Shine!'; Global shows 'Grow Up and Shine!'. Mastery wording also differs: guide 'Skill Point Bonus +3' vs game 'Training Skill Pt Gain +3'. Three-currency cost. Guide advises buying immediately for the cumulative skill point bonus.

songs.name: Seven Colors Scenery
songs.notes: First seen in a tutorial mockup, whose cost the guide confirmed; now CONFIRMED in a real offer set (set 12) at the same Vo21+Co42. The mockups do carry real data.

songs.name: Hoppity Sunny Days♪
songs.notes: In-game the name renders with a space before the note glyph ('Hoppity Sunny Days ♪').

songs.name: Precious Treasure Box
songs.notes: NAME MISMATCH: guide lists 'My Favourite Treasure Box'; Global shows 'Precious Treasure Box'.

songs.name: Fanfare for the Future!
songs.notes: Global renders it 'Fanfare for the Future!' (lower-case 'for the', trailing '!').

songs.name: Present March♪
songs.notes: In-game the name renders with a space before the note glyph ('Present March ♪').

songs.name: Make debut!
songs.notes: AWARDED 4 turns after career start, not purchased. Confirmed present in the Set List with Total Songs Learned = 1 before any song was bought - so that counter includes awards and must never be derived from purchase history.

songs.name: GIRLS' LEGEND U
songs.notes: AWARDED after Senior Year Early December with 18+ total songs learned. This is the target behind the guide's 18-song push.

offer_sets.when: after learning from set 1; unchanged across a turn boundary
offers: Zero Is Where the Center Stands!
offer_sets.learned: Zero Is Where the Center Stands!

offer_sets.when: after learning the song from set 2
after learning the technique from set 3
after learning the technique from set 4
offer_sets.note: All songs, none affordable - the state where the bot must decline and keep training.

offer_sets.when: Junior Early Aug, after several turns of training
offer_sets.note: Only the off-pattern song (Da32+Vi12) was affordable; both 21+21 songs were not. A larger total cost (44) was payable while a smaller one (42) was not - per-currency affordability is not approximable by a total.
All three matched the catalogue exactly on cost and effects; two carried different Global names than the guide.
All three affordable and all three matched the catalogue exactly.
None affordable; all three matched the catalogue.

offer_sets.when: after the 3rd concert (Classic Late Dec passed)
offer_sets.note: First set containing 3rd-concert-tier songs, confirming that tier unlocked.
None affordable (Co exhausted to 0). First REAL-offer sighting of Seven Colors Scenery, previously known only from a tutorial mockup.
All three affordable. First THREE-DIGIT balance seen (Da 138), which exposed a clipping bug in the balance OCR crop - see observations.
All three affordable. Second 3-digit balance (Da 102), read correctly by the corrected crops.

observations.guide_cross_validation: Every song observed in game matched the guide exactly on cost and effects (7/7), including one seen only in a tutorial mockup. The guide's song table is therefore treated as reliable for the 15 songs not yet encountered. Two naming differences exist - see 'Getaway! Fallin' Love' and the star/interpunct glyphs.
observations.song_costs_are_stable_per_song: 'Go This Way' appeared three times at Vo21+Co21 and 'Ring Ring Diary' twice at Pa21+Vi21. Every repeated TECHNIQUE, by contrast, changed currency. Song planning across turns is therefore sound.
observations.song_cost_shapes: Songs cost 1-3 currencies at 12/14/16/21/22/26/32/42 per currency. There is no flat song price and no reliable relationship between total cost and affordability.
observations.song_cost_includes_its_own_currency: Holds across the whole guide table: a song's cost always includes the currency matching the stat its Mastery bonus targets (Speed->Da, Stamina->Pa, Power->Vo, Guts->Vi, Wit->Co). Useful for deciding which currency to farm.
observations.songs_can_have_flat_stat_masteries: Mastery kind does not partition by lesson type. Songs may grant a flat stat ('Speed +22'), a training gain ('Training Speed Gain +1'), skill points, or a skill point bonus.
observations.concert_bonus_types: Three only: Support Chain Event Frequency Lvl +N, Specialty Priority +N, Friendship Training Effectiveness +N%. These are exactly the three fields the Concert Info panel tracks, so that screen verifies purchases landed.
observations.offer_set_behaviour: Three offers at a time, no scrolling. A set is fixed until a purchase (verified pixel-identical across a turn boundary), then immediately refreshes and the shop reopens on the new set - confirmed for both technique and song purchases. Passed-over lessons return later. Sets can be homogeneous.
observations.unlock_tiers: 8 songs from the start, +3 after the 1st concert, +4 after the 2nd, +6 after the 3rd, plus 2 awards. The guide also gates song availability behind cumulative technique counts, which is consistent with observed sets alternating between all-technique and all-song.
observations.guide_names_are_unreliable: The guide's SONG NAMES are often JP or pre-localisation titles. Of 10 songs seen in game: 3 substantively renamed (RUNxRUN -> 'Run n' Run!', A NO NE -> 'Hey, Guess What!', 'Run Away! Fallin' Love' -> 'Getaway! Fallin' Love'), 4 differ only in punctuation/case/glyph, 3 match exactly. Expect several of the still-unobserved songs to carry different Global names. COST AND EFFECTS, by contrast, have matched 10/10. Do NOT look songs up by name against this file - see 'match_by_signature_not_name'.
observations.match_by_signature_not_name: The tuple (per-currency cost + mastery effect + concert effect) is UNIQUE across all 21 purchasable songs - verified, zero collisions - so the runtime can identify an offered song from what it reads on the card and look up 'priority' without trusting the name. Cost alone is NOT unique (Zero Is Where the Center Stands!/Getaway! Fallin' Love share Da21+Vi21; Ring Ring Diary/Run For Our Dream share Pa21+Vi21), so the effect must be part of the key.
observations.balance_crop_must_fit_three_digits: Performance point balances are RIGHT-ALIGNED, so a 3-digit value extends left into the gap beside the currency chip. The original crops (Da 112-168 etc.) clipped the leading digit: Da 138 read as 38. Corrected crops, verified 80/80 across 16 shop captures including the 3-digit case: Da 98-168, Pa 221-291, Vo 344-414, Vi 467-537, Co 590-660 (x1 = 98 + 123*i, x2 = 168 + 123*i), all at y 98-150. Caps rise by 50 per concert from 200, so 3-digit balances are normal in the late career and this WILL bite otherwise.

strategy_from_guide.junior: Prioritise friendship bonds. Take Mastery stat-bonus songs over concert-bonus songs early. Secure at least three songs before the 1st concert for Great Success.
strategy_from_guide.classic: Buy 'Run For Our Dream' and 'Grow Up, Shine!' as soon as they unlock - their skill point bonuses compound over the rest of the career. Limit to about four songs per concert cycle.
strategy_from_guide.senior: Aim for 18 total songs to earn GIRLS' LEGEND U. Prefer stat Mastery bonuses over concert bonuses late, since the application window shrinks.
strategy_from_guide.races: The guide states races give zero performance points, so race only for goals and secret events. UNVERIFIED in game.
strategy_from_guide.great_success: Read the Hype gauge rather than counting songs - it is the authoritative readiness signal and resets each concert.
